title: 奉命谋杀
summary:
Following a phone call from her father, Mary Darland discusses her agitation with her husband, Philip. Her father had expressed anger and warned of consequences regarding the recent dropping of charges against Jack Aigle. Philip explains that the situation could turn the matter back into a "cold case," potentially drawing police interest. Although Mary wants to avoid gossip and the discomfort of revisiting the past, Philip insists they must meet with Marshall at "the sunny corner" to discuss their circumstances. Mary resists the idea due to Philip's leg problems and recent break-ins at their house, but Philip maintains that the family must face the situation.

Meanwhile, at a hotel in Drearymoor, Arthur Calgary reflects on his shaken experience at the Sunny Corner. He ruminates on the unexpected and painful reactions of several people, including Hester Aigle’s contemptuous rejection of his plea for fairness, the harshness of a woman named Corston, Leo Aigle’s strange lack of reaction to his son's innocence, and the overly sympathetic behavior of Leo’s secretary.

Calgary is interrupted by a phone call from a Mr. Aikman, but the visitor who arrives at his room is Michael Aigle, Leo's son. Appearing bitter and resentful, Michael tracked Calgary down by calling various hotels. Michael questions Calgary's strength and his involvement in the Hayes-Bentley Antarctic Expedition, revealing his motive is a reaction to the news regarding his brother. Michael challenges the accuracy of the alibi Calgary provided for Jack, suggesting Calgary might have been mistaken about the time. Calgary firmly defends his precision, stating that his watch and the car clock were synchronized when he picked up Jack at 6:35 and dropped him off at Dreyfus at 7:35.
